summary: postinstall exfiltrates host info and file contents to Telegram bot API
details: The package's postinstall.js runs automatically on `npm install` and performs credential/host-reconnaissance exfiltration. The script imports `fs`, `os`, `https`, and `http`; collects host identifiers via `os.hostname()` and `os.userInfo()`; reads local files via `fs.readFileSync` (postinstall.js lines 49 and 58); and issues `https.request` POST calls to `https://api.telegram.org` (lines 14/15 and 32/33) — a hardcoded attacker-controlled Telegram bot endpoint used as the exfiltration channel. The combination of automatic install-time execution, host reconnaissance, filesystem reads, and POST to a hardcoded Telegram bot API constitutes installer-side data theft on any machine that runs `npm install` for this package. The `polygon-sdk` name and `wagni_bot` scope suggest a lure impersonating a legitimate blockchain SDK.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
